title: Editor release history
description: Per-fork history of observed editor releases and their embedded VS Code engine versions. The daily editor-release-monitor workflow maintains one of these per tracked fork (e.g. cursor-release-history.json, vscodium-release-history.json). The weekly vscode-extension-engine-bump analyzer reads every *-release-history.json under .github/, filters each by released_at, takes the min vscode_version across forks, and opens a bump issue when that min exceeds the current engines.vscode floor.

fork_name.description: Display name of the fork used verbatim in issue bodies. Examples: 'Cursor', 'VSCodium', 'Windsurf'.

releases.description: Append-only log of observed releases. Order is not enforced but entries are typically appended chronologically. Entries older than 2 years are pruned by the monitor on each run.

version.description: Fork-native release version string (e.g. '0.45.14' for Cursor, '1.105.0' for VSCodium). Identity for deduping; opaque to the analyzer.

released_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp marking when the fork shipped this release. Used by the analyzer for the 30-day grace check.

vscode_version.description: VS Code engine version embedded in this release, extracted from the fork's product.json or equivalent. Authoritative for the analyzer's floor computation.

source_url.description: Optional audit-only URL the monitor used to obtain this release's data (e.g. an AppImage path, a raw GitHub URL). Not consumed by the analyzer.
